title: General Dhiraj Seth Takes Charge as 30th Chief of Army Staff
summary: General Dhiraj Seth assumed charge as the new Chief of Army Staff on June 30, 2026, succeeding General Upendra Dwivedi. The handover ceremony marked a transition in India's top military leadership. Outgoing Chief General Dwivedi noted that future wars will be more integrated and theatre-oriented in nature, signalling the direction of India's military modernisation.
exam_angle: Appointments to constitutional/statutory posts and senior military leadership are frequently tested in UPSC Prelims. The shift to theatre commands is a key defence reform topic for GS Paper 3.
key_facts: New Army Chief: General Dhiraj Seth
Outgoing Army Chief: General Upendra Dwivedi
Effective date: June 30, 2026
Outgoing chief's key remark: Future wars to be integrated and theatre-oriented

title: India Semiconductor Mission 2.0 Gets Rs 1.25 Lakh Crore Clearance from Finance Ministry Panel
summary: A Finance Ministry panel cleared a Rs 1.25 lakh crore (approximately Rs 1.25 trillion) outlay for India Semiconductor Mission 2.0, paving the way for India to deepen its semiconductor manufacturing ecosystem. The clearance signals a major policy push to reduce import dependence and position India as a global chip manufacturing hub. The original India Semiconductor Mission was launched in 2021 with a Rs 76,000 crore incentive package.
exam_angle: Semiconductor self-reliance is a core GS Paper 3 theme linking industrial policy, technology sovereignty, and import substitution. The scale of outlay and its approval body are key facts for Prelims.
key_facts: Scheme: India Semiconductor Mission 2.0
Approved outlay: Rs 1.25 lakh crore
Cleared by: Finance Ministry panel
Objective: Advance India's semiconductor fabrication and chip design ecosystem

title: Delhi EV Policy 2026 Cleared: Focus on Two-Wheelers and Legacy Vehicle Transition
summary: Delhi's Electric Vehicle Policy 2026 (EV Policy 2.0) has been officially cleared, with a significant emphasis on incentivising two-wheelers and managing the transition of legacy internal combustion engine two-wheelers and repair shops. The policy bets big on the two-wheeler segment as the primary driver of EV adoption given its dominance in Delhi's vehicle mix. It also addresses the impact on existing repair shop ecosystems during the EV transition.
exam_angle: State EV policies linked to national EV targets (FAME scheme, Net Zero commitments) are relevant for GS Paper 3 (Environment and Economy). Delhi's policy is particularly significant given the city's air quality challenges.
key_facts: Policy: Delhi EV Policy 2026 (EV Policy 2.0)
Key focus: Two-wheeler EV adoption and legacy vehicle transition
Cleared by: Delhi government
Context: Delhi's severe air pollution and national EV adoption targets

title: Pakistan Threatens India Over Indus Waters Treaty as Tensions Escalate
summary: A Pakistani minister issued a strong warning to India over the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT), amid ongoing tensions following India's suspension of the treaty after the Pahalgam terror attack in April 2025. Pakistan's Indus Commissioner stated he had written to his Indian counterpart four times over fluctuations in Chenab river flows. India had placed the IWT in abeyance citing cross-border terrorism, marking one of the most significant disruptions to the 1960 treaty.
exam_angle: The Indus Waters Treaty (brokered by World Bank in 1960) is a perennial GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 topic. India's suspension and Pakistan's response connect to concepts of treaty obligations, transboundary water disputes, and diplomatic signalling.
key_facts: Treaty: Indus Waters Treaty, signed 1960, brokered by World Bank
India suspended/placed IWT in abeyance post-Pahalgam attack (April 2025)
Rivers in dispute: Chenab (western river allocated to Pakistan)
Pakistan's Indus Commissioner wrote 4 times to Indian counterpart over Chenab fluctuations

title: US-India Trade Deal in Final Steps: US Ambassador Sergio Gor
summary: US Ambassador to India Sergio Gor stated that the bilateral trade deal between the United States and India is in its final stages, with only about 1% of negotiations remaining. Indian-American Congressman Ro Khanna simultaneously warned that the US-India relationship is at its lowest point in 30 years due to Trump administration policies. The trade deal, if concluded, would be a landmark in bilateral economic ties between the world's largest democracies.
exam_angle: Bilateral trade negotiations, tariff structures, and US-India economic relations are key GS Paper 2 (IR) topics. The contrast between diplomatic optimism and congressional criticism reflects the complexity of the relationship.
key_facts: US Ambassador to India: Sergio Gor
Status: ~1% of trade deal negotiations remaining
Ro Khanna (Indian-American Democrat): US-India ties at lowest in 30 years
Context: Trump-era tariff disputes and bilateral trade deficit tensions

title: US-Iran Nuclear Talks: Qatar Confirms No Direct Negotiations in Doha; US Envoys Meet Mediators
summary: Qatar confirmed that no direct US-Iran talks would take place in Doha, even as US special envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner met with Qatari mediators. Iranian officials engaged with Qatari intermediaries on issues including frozen funds. Qatar continues to play a key mediating role in West Asia diplomacy involving the US, Iran, and ongoing Gaza-related negotiations.
exam_angle: Iran nuclear diplomacy and Qatar's role as a regional mediator are important for GS Paper 2 (IR) and current geopolitical awareness. The involvement of US envoys and indirect talks format is exam-relevant.
key_facts: No direct US-Iran talks in Doha confirmed by Qatar
US envoys: Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner
Qatar's role: Key intermediary/mediator in US-Iran and West Asia diplomacy
Issues on table: Frozen Iranian funds, nuclear programme

title: Kotak Mahindra Bank to Acquire Deutsche Bank's India Retail and Wealth Business for Rs 282 Crore
summary: Kotak Mahindra Bank announced the acquisition of Deutsche Bank's India retail banking and wealth management business for approximately Rs 282 crore (about $30 million). The deal marks Deutsche Bank's exit from the Indian retail banking segment while strengthening Kotak's retail and wealth management portfolio. The acquisition is subject to regulatory approvals.
exam_angle: Banking sector consolidation, foreign bank exits, and RBI's role in approving bank acquisitions are relevant GS Paper 3 (Economy) themes. Kotak Mahindra Bank is a systemically important private bank in India.
key_facts: Acquirer: Kotak Mahindra Bank
Seller: Deutsche Bank (India retail and wealth business)
Deal value: Rs 282 crore (~$30 million)
Significance: Deutsche Bank exits Indian retail banking segment

title: Government Extends Import Duty Exemption on Key Petrochemical Products Till July 15
summary: The Indian government extended the import duty exemption on key petrochemical products until July 15, 2026. The extension is aimed at maintaining domestic supply chain stability and keeping input costs for downstream industries in check. Petrochemicals are critical feedstocks for plastics, fertilisers, and a range of manufacturing sectors.
exam_angle: Import duty exemptions, trade policy, and their impact on domestic industries are GS Paper 3 topics. This connects to topics of industrial policy, custom duties, and the role of the Finance Ministry in tariff management.
key_facts: Measure: Extension of import duty exemption on key petrochemical products
Extended till: July 15, 2026
Objective: Stabilise domestic supply chains and manage input costs
Ministry involved: Finance Ministry (customs/tariff policy)

title: Finance Ministry's June Review Warns Water Scarcity May Top India's Risk Buffer List
summary: The Finance Ministry's June 2026 monthly economic review issued a new warning that water scarcity may soon top India's risk buffer list, surpassing traditional macroeconomic concerns. The report highlights the growing threat of water stress to agricultural productivity, industrial output, and overall economic resilience. This is a significant policy signal linking environmental sustainability to fiscal and economic planning.
exam_angle: Water scarcity as an economic and fiscal risk connects GS Paper 1 (Geography/Environment) with GS Paper 3 (Economy). Finance Ministry economic reviews are primary sources for UPSC current affairs.
key_facts: Source: Finance Ministry June 2026 monthly economic review
Warning: Water scarcity may top India's risk buffer list
Implication: Threat to agriculture, industry, and economic resilience
Significance: First time FinMin formally flags water as top macro risk

title: Europe Heatwave 2026: Record Temperatures in Slovakia and Czechia; Roads Melt
summary: A severe heatwave swept across Eastern Europe in late June 2026, with Slovakia and Czechia recording record-breaking temperatures. The World Meteorological Organization (WMO) noted the unprecedented scale of the heat event, with reports of roads melting and tram tracks buckling in affected cities. The heatwave is consistent with the broader global trend of increasing frequency and intensity of extreme heat events driven by climate change.
exam_angle: Extreme weather events linked to climate change, the role of the WMO, and global warming impacts are important for GS Paper 3 (Environment) and GS Paper 1 (Geography). Such events reinforce the science behind IPCC findings.
key_facts: Affected region: Eastern Europe (Slovakia, Czechia worst affected)
Authority flagging event: World Meteorological Organization (WMO)
Impact: Record temperatures, road damage, infrastructure stress
Context: Part of global trend of intensifying extreme heat events due to climate change

title: Ethanol Blending Programme: Government Tells Supreme Court E20 is an Experiment, Results Expected Next Year
summary: The Indian government informed the Supreme Court that the Ethanol Blending Programme (EBP), specifically the E20 target (20% ethanol blending in petrol), is being treated as an experiment and that results will be available by next year. The submission came amid concerns raised before the court about the environmental and engine-related impact of E20 fuel. India has been progressively scaling up ethanol blending as part of its energy transition and agricultural support strategy.
exam_angle: The Ethanol Blending Programme connects GS Paper 3 topics of energy policy, agriculture (sugarcane/maize use), environment, and judicial oversight of government schemes. Supreme Court's engagement with executive policy is also a GS Paper 2 theme.
key_facts: Programme: Ethanol Blending Programme (EBP) — E20 target (20% ethanol in petrol)
Government position before SC: E20 is an experiment; results expected in one year
National target: 20% ethanol blending by 2025 (revised timelines under discussion)
Significance: Connects to energy security, farmers' income, and net-zero commitments

title: HDFC Bank Appoints Former CEC Rajiv Kumar as Chairman
summary: HDFC Bank, India's largest private sector bank, appointed former Chief Election Commissioner (CEC) Rajiv Kumar as its new Chairman. Rajiv Kumar served as the 24th Chief Election Commissioner of India before retiring. His appointment to a top corporate board is notable given his background in civil services and election administration.
exam_angle: Appointments to boards of systemically important banks and the careers of former constitutional functionaries (such as the CEC) are relevant for GS Paper 2 (Polity) and Persons in News for Prelims.
key_facts: Appointee: Rajiv Kumar (former Chief Election Commissioner of India)
New role: Chairman, HDFC Bank
HDFC Bank: India's largest private sector bank
Rajiv Kumar was the 24th Chief Election Commissioner of India

title: Amarnath Yatra 2026: Army Deploys Anti-Drone Arsenal, Radars and Anti-Aircraft Guns for Pilgrim Security
summary: The Indian Army deployed a multi-layered security grid for the Amarnath Yatra 2026, including anti-drone systems, surveillance radars, and anti-aircraft guns along the pilgrimage route in Jammu & Kashmir. The J&K Lieutenant Governor performed the 'Pratham Puja' to ceremonially mark the commencement of the Yatra. The security deployment reflects heightened vigilance in the region following recent cross-border tensions.
exam_angle: Security arrangements for sensitive pilgrimage routes, the role of the Army in internal security, and drone threat mitigation are GS Paper 3 (Internal Security) topics. The Amarnath Yatra is also a Places in News item.
key_facts: Event: Amarnath Yatra 2026 commencement
Security deployed: Anti-drone systems, surveillance radars, anti-aircraft guns
Ceremonial start: 'Pratham Puja' performed by J&K Lieutenant Governor
Location: Jammu & Kashmir

title: National Statistics Day 2026: NSO Celebrates 20th Edition in Mumbai
summary: The National Statistics Office (NSO) celebrated the 20th National Statistics Day on June 29, 2026, in Mumbai. National Statistics Day is observed annually on June 29, the birth anniversary of Prof. P.C. Mahalanobis, the father of Indian statistics and founder of the Indian Statistical Institute. The day aims to promote the use of statistics in policymaking and public administration.
exam_angle: National Statistics Day, the NSO, and Prof. P.C. Mahalanobis are standard GS Paper 3 (Economy/Data) and Persons in News topics. The NSO is the primary government body for economic data collection and GDP estimation.
key_facts: Event: 20th National Statistics Day, June 29, 2026
Organised by: National Statistics Office (NSO)
Day observed on: June 29 — birth anniversary of Prof. P.C. Mahalanobis
Prof. Mahalanobis: Founder of Indian Statistical Institute (ISI), Kolkata; architect of India's statistical system

title: RBI's New Funding Rules Impact Indian Proprietary Trading Firms
summary: New funding rules issued by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) have delivered a significant blow to Indian proprietary trading firms by restricting certain funding avenues used for leveraged trading operations. The regulations are aimed at tightening financial sector risk management and reducing systemic vulnerabilities associated with leveraged positions in capital markets. Industry stakeholders have described the impact as a 'body blow' to the prop-trading ecosystem.
exam_angle: RBI's regulatory role over capital markets participants, leverage restrictions, and financial sector risk management are GS Paper 3 (Economy) topics. RBI's macro-prudential regulations are frequently tested.
key_facts: Regulator: Reserve Bank of India (RBI)
Impact: New funding restrictions on proprietary trading firms
Purpose: Reduce systemic risk from leveraged trading in capital markets
Affected entities: Indian proprietary (prop) trading firms

title: China Seen as Strategic Winner from US Military Involvement in Middle East: Report
summary: A report by The Guardian, citing strategic analysis, concluded that China is emerging as a clear geopolitical winner from the United States' military engagement in the Middle East under the Trump administration. US military focus on West Asia has diverted attention from the Indo-Pacific, allowing China to consolidate strategic and economic positions across the region. The findings have implications for India's foreign policy calculus in the Indo-Pacific.
exam_angle: The strategic interplay between US Middle East engagement and Indo-Pacific power dynamics is relevant for GS Paper 2 (IR). China's strategic gains amid US preoccupation is a key theme in contemporary international relations.
key_facts: Finding: China is the primary strategic beneficiary of US military engagement in Middle East
Context: Trump administration's military operations in West Asia (2026)
Mechanism: US distraction from Indo-Pacific allows China strategic consolidation
Implication: Shifts balance of power calculations in Indo-Pacific, relevant to India's strategic posture

title: GST Completes 9 Years: Calls for Simplification, Technology Integration in Next Decade
summary: As India's Goods and Services Tax (GST) entered its 9th year (implemented July 1, 2017), analysts and policymakers called for the next decade to focus on simplification of the rate structure, building taxpayer trust, and deeper technology integration. GST has transformed India's indirect tax architecture by subsuming over 17 central and state taxes. Key pending reforms include rationalisation of the rate slabs and inclusion of petroleum products under GST.
exam_angle: GST is a flagship constitutional amendment (101st CAA, 2016) and a core GS Paper 3 topic. Anniversary milestones, pending reforms, and the GST Council structure are standard exam themes.
key_facts: GST implemented: July 1, 2017 (9th anniversary in 2026)
Constitutional basis: 101st Constitutional Amendment Act, 2016
GST subsumed: 17+ central and state taxes
Key pending reforms: Rate rationalisation, inclusion of petroleum products

title: PoK Protesters Warn Pakistan Against Engaging India on Terms That Exclude PoK's Status
summary: Protesters in Pakistan-occupied Kashmir (PoK) publicly warned the Pakistani government against any diplomatic engagement with India that does not address PoK's political status, asserting that PoK is not legally part of Pakistan. The protests reflect internal dissent within PoK about Islamabad's authority over the region. This comes amid broader India-Pakistan tensions following Operation Sindoor and subsequent ceasefire.
exam_angle: PoK's constitutional and legal status, India's claim over the region (as part of Jammu & Kashmir per India's position), and Pakistan's internal political dynamics are important GS Paper 2 (Polity/IR) topics.
key_facts: Location: Pakistan-occupied Kashmir (PoK)
Protesters' assertion: PoK is not legally part of Pakistan
Context: Post-Operation Sindoor India-Pakistan tensions and ceasefire
India's position: Entire J&K (including PoK) is an integral part of India
